host: ubu-2 RTX 5070 sm_120 driver 580 (ptxas only -- no GPU execution)
honest_scope: Isolation probe measures ONLY the BM=32 O-accumulator pattern (16 fp32 regs per thread declared, ptxas coalesced to 12 actual physical regs due to single-warp scope + no surrounding QK/softmax live ranges). Full-kernel reg count = this isolated O-accumulator footprint PLUS R10's 91-reg tile-load/QK/softmax overhead, projected by plan section 4 to ~103 reg/thread + 0 spill. Probe confirms (a) no spill under sm_120a, (b) PTX is syntactically valid for sm_120a, (c) accumulator-direct-to-global store pattern compiles cleanly (no smem stage required). The full-kernel claim of <=255 reg/thread is unfalsified by this probe; the probe falsifies only the WORST-case scenario of catastrophic spill, which did not occur.
ptxas_raw_stderr_excerpt: ptxas info    : Compiling entry function 'probe_bm32_oreg' for 'sm_120a'
ptxas info    : Function properties for probe_bm32_oreg
0 bytes stack frame, 0 bytes spill stores, 0 bytes spill loads
ptxas info    : Used 12 registers, used 0 barriers
implication_for_bc4_round14: Risk-a CLEARED for cheap-first oracle gate. BC4 round-14 silicon fire (flash_attn_bm32_occupancy_v0) is NOT BLOCKED by reg-pressure concern at the BM=32 O-reg shape. Round-14 should proceed with __launch_bounds__(128, 3) as plan section 4 recommended; if actual full-kernel ptxas reports >255 reg or any spill, that is a separate falsifier (F-FUSION-ATTN-BM32-OCCUPANCY-WALL primary) that emerges only at full-kernel build.
